hub_subtitle: Medical English to welcome guests and present with confidence

lesson.menu_desc: Block 3 continues. Welcome a guest speaker and ask polite questions: Could you tell me...? Do you know...? Would you mind...? Start polite, then keep normal word order. A meeting dialogue, two voice messages, and three role-plays. -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Asking Politely
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Graziele Dias | Aula 19 -- Asking Politely

listenings.text: Hi Graziele, this is Doctor Carter. Before the meeting, a small favor. When I join, please welcome me and ask a few polite questions. You can say: Could you tell me your name? Do you know how many minutes you need? Would you mind sharing your screen? Keep it calm and kind. You will do great.

Hi Graziele, it is Ana. A quick tip about polite questions. To sound polite, start with Could you, Do you know, or Would you mind. Then keep normal word order: Could you tell me where the room is? Not where is the room. Softer words make a calmer meeting. Keep it simple. You will do great.

extra_audio.text: Graziele welcomes a guest speaker. First, the greeting: good morning and welcome. Then, the name: could you tell me your name, please? Next, the time: do you know how many minutes you need? After that, the screen: would you mind sharing your screen? Finally, the recording: would you mind if we record the meeting?
